signals.reasoning: Close the live COMP long. It has only retained about 11% of peak P&L, the giveback warning is urgent, and veto is active, so there is no reason to keep defending a winner that has already mostly evaporated.
Cover the live MON short immediately. The trade is back to a live loss, the board remains bullish against it, and the urgent warning means the thesis is fully broken.
BTC is steady but veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode.
ETH remains mixed and does not offer enough edge to justify action during the event brake.
SOL still has a valid squeeze-style long profile, but veto keeps it out of the buy bucket for now.
STBL remains a clean bearish fade candidate because funding is extremely positive and crowding is high, though conviction is capped by the veto regime.
MAVIA is too thin and noisy to trust as an actionable setup.
HEMI has the same problem as MAVIA: weak market quality and not enough edge to justify a trade.
ZEREBRO still lacks enough directional edge or trade quality to act on here.
HMSTR is too illiquid and noisy to be a serious setup despite mild momentum.
